overview: A series of UX improvements, WYSIWYG admin editing, full blog content support (markdown-sourced from 3-resources/posts), a functional order tray panel, AI waiter admin settings, and a comprehensive test suite. Dev server: http://localhost:3030. Each iteration must verify changes with Chrome DevTools or Playwright before marking passes: true.
goals: Make the order tray functional so guests can show their order to the human waiter
Add per-category highlighted item that admin can change via WYSIWYG on the menu page
Display blog posts with full content, sourced from markdown files in 3-resources/posts/ai-posts
Enable WYSIWYG editing for blog, about, and contact pages when admin is logged in
Add AI waiter admin settings panel linked from the chat widget
Add a Tests tab in /admin with runnable smoke/unit/integration/e2e tests
All implementations verified visually with Chrome DevTools or Playwright

userStories.title: Floating Order Tray Panel
userStories.description: When guests click '+ Add' on any menu item, the item should be added to a visible floating tray/cart panel. The tray should show all selected items with quantities and total price. Guests can show this tray to the human waiter or use it as a reference when ordering at the POS. The tray must float above other content, be dismissible, and work on mobile. The TrayContext (src/lib/tray-context.tsx) already stores items â€” we just need the visible UI panel. Also add a floating tray button (cart icon with badge count) near the chat widget button.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking '+ Add' on a menu item adds it to the tray (quantity increments if added again)
A floating cart icon button appears at bottom-left (or bottom-right near chat but non-overlapping) with a badge showing total item count
Clicking the cart icon opens a tray panel showing: item name, quantity (+/- controls), price, and total
The panel has a 'Clear Order' button and a title like 'Your Order â€” Show to Waiter'
On mobile the tray is a bottom sheet; on desktop it's a floating panel
The tray is dismissible (X button or click outside)
Verify in Chrome DevTools: click Add â†’ badge count updates, open tray â†’ items visible
technicalNotes: TrayContext already in src/lib/tray-context.tsx with addItem/removeItem/clearTray/totalPrice
Create src/components/menu/tray-panel.tsx â€” the floating panel UI
Create src/components/menu/tray-button.tsx â€” the floating button with badge
Add TrayButton and TrayPanel to src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx alongside ChatWidget
Use Tailwind for mobile bottom-sheet vs desktop floating panel
Position: tray button at bottom-left, chat at bottom-right to avoid overlap
Use TrayContext items.reduce to calculate total count for badge

userStories.title: Per-Category Highlighted Item on Menu Page
userStories.description: On the menu page (/en/menu), each category section should have one item visually highlighted as the 'chef's pick' or 'must try'. By default, the first item in each category (by sortOrder) is highlighted. When admin is logged in, they can click any item to set it as the highlighted item for its category (WYSIWYG). The highlighted item gets a prominent badge/border treatment. The selected highlight per category is persisted in the DB (new column or separate table).
acceptanceCriteria: Each category section shows exactly one item with a prominent 'Chef's Pick' or 'â˜… Highlighted' visual treatment (gold border or badge)
Default highlighted item is the lowest sort_order item in the category
When admin is logged in, hovering an item shows a 'Set as Highlight' button
Clicking 'Set as Highlight' immediately updates the UI and persists to DB via POST /api/admin/highlights
Non-admin users never see the 'Set as Highlight' button
Highlighted state survives page reload
Verify in Chrome DevTools: admin clicks set â†’ highlight updates without full page reload
technicalNotes: Add a new DB table `category_highlights` (category TEXT PRIMARY KEY, item_id TEXT) or a column `highlight_for_category` on menu_items
Create API route src/app/api/admin/highlights/route.ts (GET + POST, admin-auth required)
Update MenuGrid or MenuCard to accept isHighlighted prop and render visual treatment
Add admin click handler in editable-menu-card.tsx or MenuCard when isAdmin=true
Pass highlightedByCategory map from menu page server component to MenuGrid
Use a gold ring or amber badge: className='ring-2 ring-amber-400' on the card wrapper

userStories.title: Blog Page with Full Article Layout
userStories.description: The blog page (/en/blog) currently shows only a card grid with title and excerpt. Redesign it to look like a proper blog listing: a featured/hero post at the top (full-width with large image and excerpt), then a grid of smaller cards below. Each card should show cover image, title, excerpt (2-3 lines), tags, and publish date. Clicking a post goes to a full article page at /en/blog/[slug].
acceptanceCriteria: Blog listing page has a featured post section at the top (full-width) with large image, title, and first 3 lines of excerpt
Below the featured post, remaining posts appear in a 2-column card grid
Each card shows: cover image (aspect-video), tags (pill badges), title, 2-line excerpt, and date
Cards have hover effects (shadow lift, slight scale on image)
Blog detail page (/en/blog/[slug]) shows full article content with proper typography
Verify in browser at http://localhost:3030/en/blog: featured post visible, grid below it
technicalNotes: Redesign src/app/[locale]/blog/page.tsx â€” split posts into featured (first) + rest
Update src/components/blog/post-card.tsx for the card variant
Create src/components/blog/featured-post.tsx for the hero section
Blog detail page already exists at src/app/[locale]/blog/[slug]/page.tsx â€” ensure it renders content
Use prose typography class from Tailwind for article body

userStories.title: Import Markdown Blog Posts from 3-resources/posts
userStories.description: Blog posts currently only come from Notion. The ai-posts folder at C:\Users\Jyue\Documents\2-areas\makan-moments-cafe\3-resources\posts\ai-posts contains rich markdown blog posts. Import these into the website's blog system by: (1) copying them to a content/blog/ folder inside the project, and (2) updating src/lib/blog.ts to read from both Notion AND local markdown files (merging the two sources). The markdown posts contain full content, tags, and dates in their frontmatter/filename.
acceptanceCriteria: A content/blog/ directory exists in the project root with markdown post files copied from 3-resources/posts/ai-posts
The blog listing page shows posts from both Notion and markdown files
Each markdown post renders with its full content on the detail page /en/blog/[slug]
Posts are sorted by date (newest first) regardless of source
Markdown post frontmatter parsed for: title, date, tags, excerpt (first paragraph after ---)
Verify: http://localhost:3030/en/blog shows markdown posts; click one â†’ full content displayed
technicalNotes: Copy markdown files from C:\Users\Jyue\Documents\2-areas\makan-moments-cafe\3-resources\posts\ai-posts to project root: content/blog/
Install gray-matter for frontmatter parsing: npm install gray-matter
Install marked or remark for markdown â†’ HTML: npm install marked
Create src/lib/blog-local.ts with readLocalPosts() function using fs.readdirSync on content/blog/
Update src/lib/blog.ts getBlogPosts() to merge Notion posts + local markdown posts
Slug = filename without .md, date from filename prefix YYYY-MM-DD or frontmatter
For the detail page, parse full markdown body to HTML and render with dangerouslySetInnerHTML (safe, admin-controlled content)
Add content/blog/ to .gitignore if posts are too large, otherwise commit them

userStories.title: Admin WYSIWYG Blog Editor
userStories.description: After admin login, the blog listing page (/en/blog) should show an edit banner and allow admin to click 'Edit' on any post to edit it inline. The blog detail page (/en/blog/[slug]) should allow direct inline editing of the title and body content. Use a simple rich-text approach: show a textarea or contenteditable with markdown, and save via API. This is WYSIWYG in the sense that edits are saved and immediately reflect on the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin edit banner appears on /en/blog when logged in (same pattern as menu page)
Each post card shows an 'Edit' button when admin is logged in
Blog detail page shows an edit mode button for admin: clicking it makes title and content editable
Changes auto-save or have a 'Save' button that calls PATCH /api/admin/blog/[slug]
After saving, the page refreshes/revalidates and shows the updated content
Non-admin users see no editing UI
Verify: admin clicks edit on a post â†’ edits title â†’ saves â†’ page shows new title
technicalNotes: Add isAdmin prop to blog listing and detail pages (via cookies check in server component, same pattern as menu page)
Create API route src/app/api/admin/blog/[slug]/route.ts with PATCH handler (admin-auth required)
For local markdown posts: PATCH handler writes updated frontmatter + body back to content/blog/{slug}.md
For Notion posts: PATCH handler calls Notion API to update page content
In blog detail page: add AdminBlogEditor client component that wraps title/content with contenteditable when isAdmin
Use revalidatePath('/[locale]/blog') after save to refresh the cached pages

userStories.title: Admin WYSIWYG About Page Editor
userStories.description: After admin login, the about page (/en/about) should show an edit banner and allow inline editing of the page content (story text, team info, values, etc.). Text blocks should become contenteditable when admin clicks 'Edit'. Changes persist to a local JSON or markdown file (content/about.md). The page re-renders with updated content after save.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin edit banner appears at top of /en/about when admin is logged in
Clicking 'Edit Mode' makes text sections contenteditable (title, paragraphs, sections)
A floating 'Save Changes' button appears in edit mode
Saving calls POST /api/admin/content/about and writes to content/about.md
After saving, the page shows the updated content without a full reload
Non-admin users see no editing UI â€” the page looks identical to current
Verify: admin edits a paragraph on about page, saves, refreshes â†’ new text persists
technicalNotes: Read the current src/app/[locale]/about/page.tsx to understand current content structure
Create content/about.md (markdown with sections) that the about page reads for its text content
Update about/page.tsx to read from content/about.md via fs (server component)
Create src/components/admin/admin-page-editor.tsx â€” generic WYSIWYG editor wrapper component
Create API route src/app/api/admin/content/[page]/route.ts with POST handler
The page editor uses contenteditable divs for each text block, tracks changes in state
Add isAdmin check via cookies in about/page.tsx (same pattern as menu page)
Inject AdminPageEditor only when isAdmin=true

userStories.title: AI Waiter Admin Settings Panel
userStories.description: After admin login, the chat widget (bottom-right floating button) should show a small gear/settings icon. Clicking it opens an AI waiter settings panel (modal or page at /admin/chat-settings) where admin can configure: system prompt text, AI model selection (Groq vs OpenRouter), response style, and view recent conversation logs. The interface should feel similar to an AI assistant management panel (like in the digiman project's rainbow AI assistant).
acceptanceCriteria: When admin is logged in, a small âš™ gear icon appears on or near the chat widget button
Clicking the gear opens a settings panel (full-page modal or /admin/chat-settings route)
Settings panel shows: current system prompt (editable textarea), model selector (Groq / OpenRouter), temperature slider
A 'Save Settings' button persists changes to a settings file or DB
The AI chat API reads these settings instead of hardcoded values
An optional 'Recent Conversations' section shows last 10 chat sessions (if logging exists)
Verify: admin edits system prompt, saves, sends a chat message â†’ AI responds with updated persona
technicalNotes: Check if admin is logged in by calling GET /api/admin/verify from the chat widget client component
Add conditional gear icon render in src/components/chat/chat-widget.tsx when isAdmin
Create src/app/admin/chat-settings/page.tsx for the settings page (admin-protected)
Create src/app/api/admin/chat-settings/route.ts (GET + POST) for reading/writing settings
Store settings in a local JSON file: data/chat-settings.json (gitignored)
Update src/lib/chat/provider.ts to read from chat-settings.json at startup
Update src/lib/chat/system-prompt.ts to accept a configurable prompt prefix
Link gear icon to /admin/chat-settings using next/navigation router.push

userStories.title: Admin WYSIWYG Contact Page Editor
userStories.description: After admin login, the contact page (/en/contact) should show an edit banner and allow inline editing of the contact information: address, phone, hours, Google Maps embed URL, and any other text content. Changes persist to content/contact.md or a DB entry. Pattern is identical to the About page editor (US-006).
acceptanceCriteria: Admin edit banner appears at top of /en/contact when admin is logged in
Clicking 'Edit Mode' makes contact fields editable (address, phone, hours, map URL, description)
A floating 'Save Changes' button appears in edit mode
Saving calls POST /api/admin/content/contact and writes to content/contact.md

Non-admin users see no editing UI
Verify: admin changes opening hours, saves, refreshes â†’ new hours displayed

Create content/contact.md with structured contact fields (YAML-like frontmatter)
Update contact/page.tsx to read from content/contact.md (server component)
Reuse AdminPageEditor component created in US-006
Reuse API route src/app/api/admin/content/[page]/route.ts â€” 'contact' as [page] param
Fields to make editable: address, phone, email, hours (per day), map embed URL, tagline

userStories.title: Admin Tests Tab with Programmatic Test Suite
userStories.description: Add a 'Tests' tab to the /admin panel (alongside Menu, Categories, Rules, Blog). This tab shows a list of tests (smoke, unit, integration, e2e) with a 'Run All' button and individual 'Run' buttons per test. Tests run programmatically via API calls and return pass/fail status with output logs. Test results are displayed in real-time. Passing tests can auto-generate new user stories to insert into prd.json.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Tests' tab appears in /admin alongside the existing Menu/Categories/Rules/Blog tabs
The Tests tab shows a categorized list: Smoke Tests, Unit Tests, Integration Tests, E2E Tests
Each test has a name, description, status (pending/running/pass/fail), and output log
'Run All' button runs all tests sequentially and shows live status updates
Individual 'Run' button runs a single test
Test results show: total passed, failed, duration
A 'Generate Stories' button creates new prd.json entries for failing tests
Verify: click 'Run All' â†’ tests run â†’ results display with pass/fail status
technicalNotes: Add 'Tests' to TABS array in src/components/admin/admin-tabs.tsx
Create src/components/admin/admin-tests-panel.tsx
Create src/app/api/admin/tests/route.ts â€” GET (list tests), POST (run test by id)
Create src/lib/tests/ directory with test definitions:
- smoke.ts: check homepage 200, menu page 200, blog page 200, contact page 200, about page 200
- unit.ts: test menu data parsing, blog post slug generation, price formatting, tray context logic
- integration.ts: test API routes (GET /api/admin/menu, GET /api/admin/blog), DB connection, auth flow
- e2e.ts: use Playwright to test add-to-tray flow, admin login, menu filter, chat widget open
Each test is a function: async () => { steps, assertions, return { pass: boolean, log: string } }
API POST /api/admin/tests runs the test and streams results using TransformStream
Install @playwright/test if not already installed; use headless Chrome for e2e tests
Generate stories button: POST to /api/admin/tests/generate-stories â€” reads failing tests and appends to prd.json

userStories.title: Chrome DevTools Verification for All Features
userStories.description: After implementing each feature, use Chrome DevTools (via MCP or Playwright) to verify the implementation has no console errors, no network failures, and renders correctly on mobile (390px) and desktop (1280px). This is a quality gate story â€” it does not add new features but ensures all previous stories are working in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigate to http://localhost:3030/en â€” no console errors, hero image loads within 3s
Navigate to http://localhost:3030/en/menu â€” click Add on 3 items â†’ tray badge shows 3 â†’ open tray â†’ items listed correctly
Navigate to http://localhost:3030/en/blog â€” featured post visible, grid below it, click a markdown post â†’ full content renders
Navigate to http://localhost:3030/en/about â€” page renders without errors
Navigate to http://localhost:3030/en/contact â€” page renders without errors
Log in as admin (POST /api/admin/login) â€” menu edit banner visible, blog edit buttons visible
Open chat widget â†’ gear icon visible (admin) â†’ clicking opens settings panel
Navigate to http://localhost:3030/admin â†’ Tests tab visible â†’ Run All â†’ at least smoke tests pass
No 404 image errors, no hydration mismatches, no TypeScript build errors (rtk tsc check)
Mobile (390px): tray button not overlapping chat button, no horizontal scroll
technicalNotes: Use Chrome DevTools MCP tools: mcp__chrome-devtools__navigate_page, mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot, mcp__chrome-devtools__list_console_messages
Or use agent-browser skill / webapp-testing skill for Playwright automation
Run rtk tsc to check for TypeScript errors before marking passes: true
Run rtk next build to ensure no build errors
For each prior story (US-001 through US-009), re-verify the acceptance criteria using browser tools
If any test fails, create a bug fix sub-story or fix inline before marking this story done

userStories.title: Chef's Pick Item Displayed Bigger at Top of Each Category
userStories.description: On the menu page, the highlighted/Chef's Pick item for each category should appear as a prominent hero card at the top of its category section — larger than regular cards, with a full-width or wider layout. Regular items follow below in the standard grid. This makes the featured dish immediately obvious and visually striking.
acceptanceCriteria: Each category section starts with the Chef's Pick item displayed as a wide/tall hero card (full-width on mobile, 2-column span on desktop)
The Chef's Pick card is visually larger: bigger image (aspect-[16/9] or [4/3] full-width), larger text, and a gold '★ Chef's Pick' badge
Regular items appear in the standard 2-3 column grid below the hero card
If no Chef's Pick is set for a category, the first item is used as default hero
Admin can still click 'Set as Highlight' on any card to change the Chef's Pick
Verify on mobile (390px): hero card full-width, no overflow
technicalNotes: Modify src/components/menu/menu-grid.tsx — for each category, separate first highlighted item from rest
Create src/components/menu/chef-pick-card.tsx — a larger variant of MenuCard
Chef pick card: full-width image (w-full aspect-video), larger font, gold ring + badge
Regular items below: standard grid (grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3)
Pass isChefPick prop to card component for visual differentiation

userStories.title: Mobile-First Bottom Navigation: Category Filter and Search at Bottom
userStories.description: On mobile, the category filter tabs and search bar on the menu page should be docked to the bottom of the screen (thumb-friendly zone) instead of the top. This follows mobile-first best practices where primary interactive controls are within thumb reach. On desktop (md+), keep the current top layout.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (< 768px): category tabs and search bar appear as a sticky bottom bar
The bottom bar is above the tray widget button (z-index layered correctly)
On desktop (md+): category tabs and search remain at top as currently
Bottom bar has a subtle background blur (backdrop-blur) for readability over content
Category filter chips in the bottom bar are horizontally scrollable
No content is hidden behind the bottom bar (body has padding-bottom equal to bar height)
Verify at 390px: categories visible at bottom, scrollable, content not hidden
technicalNotes: Modify src/components/menu/menu-filter.tsx — add 'fixed bottom-[calc(4rem+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))] left-0 right-0' on mobile, remove on md+
Wrap with 'md:relative md:bottom-auto md:sticky md:top-0' for desktop fallback
Add 'pb-32 md:pb-0' to the menu page container to prevent content hidden behind sticky bar
Use 'overflow-x-auto' on the category chips row for horizontal scrolling
Ensure z-index: 40 (below tray button z-50 and chat z-50)

userStories.title: Mobile Header: Language Selector Beside Hamburger, Replace Dark/Light Toggle
userStories.description: On mobile, the header currently shows a dark/light toggle. Replace it with the language selector (EN/MS/ZH) positioned beside the hamburger menu icon. The dark/light toggle can still be accessible via the mobile menu drawer or a secondary location, but language selection takes priority since some customers cannot read the page without switching language first.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (< 768px): header shows [Logo] [spacer] [Language Selector] [Hamburger]
The dark/light mode toggle is moved inside the mobile drawer menu or removed from mobile header
Language selector in mobile header is compact (shows current locale code or flag icon)
Tapping language selector opens a dropdown/sheet with EN/MS/ZH options
On desktop (md+): header layout unchanged — all items in nav bar
Verify at 390px: language selector visible beside hamburger, not dark/light toggle
technicalNotes: Read src/components/layout/header.tsx to understand current mobile header structure
Move ThemeToggle from mobile header to inside the mobile nav drawer
Add compact LocaleSwitcher in mobile header (before hamburger button)
Use short locale labels: 'EN', 'MY', 'ZH' or flag emoji: 🇬🇧/🇲🇾/🇨🇳
Ensure it does not overlap with Logo or push content off-screen

userStories.title: Language Selector Icons for Each Language
userStories.description: Replace text-only language labels (EN/MS/ZH) with icon-based selectors. Use flag emojis or SVG flag icons: 🇬🇧 for English, 🇲🇾 for Bahasa Malaysia, 🇨🇳 for Chinese. Show the flag icon alongside or instead of the text label. Works in both mobile header (US-013) and desktop nav.
acceptanceCriteria: Language switcher shows flag icons: 🇬🇧 EN, 🇲🇾 MY, 🇨🇳 ZH (or equivalent SVG flags)
Current active language is visually indicated (bold, underline, or highlighted)
Flag icons are accessible (aria-label on each option)
Works in mobile header compact mode AND desktop nav dropdown
Consistent styling across light/dark themes
Verify: flag icons visible and correctly mapped to each language
technicalNotes: Use flag emojis (🇬🇧 🇲🇾 🇨🇳) — works cross-platform without external deps
Or use country-flag-icons npm package for SVG flags
Update locale switcher component (find in src/components/layout/header.tsx or a dedicated locale-switcher.tsx)
Add aria-label='Switch to English' etc. for accessibility

userStories.title: Hero Image Improvements: Better Food Photos + Sharp Compression
userStories.description: The landing page hero section shows food images on the right side (see Image #2). The current images include a building shot showing other businesses (EasyAds, BaiLee above our cafe), which looks unprofessional. Replace with high-quality food photos: use the named menu photos from 3-resources/photos/Frequently used (pineapple fried rice, seafood tomyum, etc.). Compress all hero images with Sharp for fast loading. Remove repetition — each hero slot should show a unique dish.

For white background removal: use sharp with flatten(false) then threshold the white pixels, or just save as PNG and rely on CSS mix-blend-mode: multiply for light bg, or use CSS filter: invert for dark mode

Update src/components/layout/footer.tsx similarly

userStories.title: Green Theme Using Logo Colors + Impeccable Tool
userStories.description: The current website uses a warm terracotta/red color scheme. Update the primary theme color to use green (inspired by the logo.jpg colors). Use the /impeccable skill to gather design context, then update the Tailwind CSS variables in globals.css. The green should be earthy/natural (not neon), matching the cafe's botanical/organic aesthetic. Apply consistently across buttons, active states, borders, and accents.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary color is changed from red/terracotta to a natural green (e.g., OKLCH equivalent of #2d6a4f or similar forest green)
All primary buttons, active nav links, and accent elements use the new green
The change is in src/app/globals.css CSS variables (--primary, --primary-foreground etc.)
Both light and dark mode green variants are defined
The green complements the earthy/linen background — not clashing
Verify: /en homepage — View Menu button is green, active nav item is green
technicalNotes: Run /teach-impeccable skill first to gather design context for this project (saves to .ai/impeccable.md or similar)
Read src/app/globals.css to understand current OKLCH color variables
Change --primary in :root from current red OKLCH to green OKLCH: e.g., oklch(0.42 0.14 145) (dark forest green)
Change --primary-foreground to near-white: oklch(0.98 0.01 145)
For dark mode .dark: adjust to a slightly lighter green: oklch(0.55 0.16 145)
Test on both light and dark modes in Chrome DevTools
Also check logo.jpg colors with sharp to extract dominant color if needed

userStories.title: Menu Add Button: 'Added' State with Background Color Change
userStories.description: When a user clicks '+ Add' on a menu item, the button text should change to '✓ Added' and the menu card's background should subtly change color (e.g., a light green tint) to indicate the item is in the tray. If the user adds again (quantity increase), the button briefly flashes. Removing the item from the tray reverts the card to normal.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking '+ Add' immediately changes the button to '✓ Added' (or with a checkmark icon)
The button background changes from primary/10 to a green/teal to indicate 'in tray' state
The menu card background gets a subtle tint (e.g., bg-green-50/50 or ring-2 ring-green-400/30)
Adding again (tapping Added again) increments quantity and briefly pulses the button
Removing the item from the tray panel reverts the card to normal '+ Add' state
This uses the TrayContext to determine if an item is in the tray
Verify: add item → card turns tinted, button shows 'Added'; remove from tray → card reverts
technicalNotes: In src/components/menu/menu-card.tsx: use useTray() to get items, check if item.code is in items array
const isInTray = items.some(i => i.id === item.code)
Conditionally apply classes: card wrapper gets 'ring-2 ring-green-400/40 bg-green-50/30' when isInTray
Button gets 'bg-green-500 text-white' state when isInTray, shows checkmark + 'Added'
Add a pulse animation on quantity increment: useEffect to add/remove animate-pulse class
Add i18n keys: common.added = 'Added' / 'Ditambah' / '已加入'

userStories.title: Chat Widget Settings Button: Admin Only
userStories.description: The gear/settings button on the chat widget (added in US-007) is currently visible to all users. It should only be visible when an admin is logged in. Non-admin customers should never see the settings button — it creates confusion and is an internal tool only.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings gear icon on chat widget is NOT visible to non-admin users
Settings gear icon IS visible when admin is logged in
The admin check is done on the client side by calling GET /api/admin/verify
No flash of the settings icon for non-admin users on page load
Verify: logged-out user → no gear icon; admin logged in → gear icon visible
technicalNotes: Read src/components/chat/chat-widget.tsx — find the gear icon render
Add a useEffect that calls fetch('/api/admin/verify') on mount, sets isAdmin state
Only render the gear icon when isAdmin === true
src/app/api/admin/verify/route.ts should already exist (created in US-007); if not, create it
Use a loading state to avoid flash: don't render gear icon until verify response received

userStories.title: Set Appropriate AI Waiter System Prompt
userStories.description: Configure a proper system prompt for the AI waiter in /admin/chat-settings. The prompt should make the AI act as a friendly, knowledgeable waiter at Makan Moments Cafe — able to recommend dishes, explain the menu, answer questions about halal/dietary status, give directions, and handle small talk in English, Malay, and Chinese. The prompt should reflect the cafe's warm, soulful brand personality.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigate to /admin/chat-settings — system prompt textarea contains a well-crafted prompt
The prompt instructs the AI to: introduce itself as the Makan Moments AI waiter, recommend dishes, explain dietary info, give operating hours, and respond in customer's language
The AI should NOT make up prices — it should refer to the knowledge/ files
The prompt sets a warm, friendly, local Malaysian tone (not overly formal)
Save the prompt → chat widget responds with the updated persona
Test: open chat widget, ask 'What do you recommend?' — AI gives a relevant, warm recommendation
technicalNotes: Write the system prompt and save it to data/chat-settings.json via POST /api/admin/chat-settings

The prompt should include: role (AI waiter at Makan Moments Cafe), personality (friendly, warm, multilingual), capabilities (menu recommendations, dietary info, directions, operating hours), and limitations (no POS integration, no reservations)
Include cafe facts: Taman Impian Emas, Skudai, Johor | 11AM-11PM | No pork, No lard, Halal-friendly | Tel: 012-7088789
Language instruction: 'Reply in the same language the customer uses. Support English, Bahasa Malaysia, and Chinese (Simplified).'
Read knowledge/cafe-facts.md and knowledge/menu-knowledge.md to align with existing knowledge base

userStories.title: Admin Panel Mobile-First Redesign: No Horizontal Scroll
userStories.description: The /admin panel currently requires horizontal scrolling on mobile (see context). All admin tabs (Menu, Categories, Rules, Blog, Tests) must be redesigned to be mobile-first: no horizontal scroll, stacked layouts, and touch-friendly controls. Tables should become card lists on mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: At 390px viewport: no horizontal scrollbar on any admin tab
Menu tab: items shown as stacked cards (not a wide table) on mobile
Tables collapse to card/list layout on mobile using responsive Tailwind classes
All buttons are ≥ 44px touch targets
Admin header (title + sign out) is single row, no overflow
Tab navigation does not require horizontal scrolling (tabs wrap or use a scrollable row with scroll-snap)
Verify at 390px: all tabs navigable, content readable, no horizontal scroll
technicalNotes: Read src/components/admin/admin-menu-table.tsx and admin-rules-panel.tsx
Replace <table> layouts with responsive card list: 'hidden md:table' for table, 'md:hidden' for mobile card list
For menu items on mobile: each row becomes a card with name + price + actions stacked
Admin header: use 'flex items-center justify-between gap-2' with text truncation
Tab bar: use 'flex overflow-x-auto scroll-snap-x' with 'scroll-snap-align-start' on each tab
All action buttons: 'min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px]'

userStories.title: Admin Rules Mobile View Fix: Horizontal Rule Name + Buttons
userStories.description: In the Rules tab on mobile (see Image #3), the rule name 'Close Coffee & Tea after 7pm (except Lemon Tea)' is stacking vertically word-by-word because it's in a narrow column beside the status badges. The layout cuts off the Active/Edit/Delete buttons on the right. Fix: show the full rule name on one line (or wrap cleanly), then show the status + action buttons on a separate line below, all within the card width.
acceptanceCriteria: Rule name displays horizontally (wrapping only when necessary, not stacking word-by-word)
Active/Disable badges and Edit/Delete buttons appear below the rule name, not beside it in a cramped column
No content is cut off at the right edge
Each rule card fits within 390px with comfortable padding
Actions (Active, Edit, Delete) are full-width or at least 44px touch target
Verify at 390px: rule name readable, buttons visible and tappable
technicalNotes: Read src/components/admin/admin-rules-panel.tsx
Current layout likely uses flex-row with name on left, badges on right — causing name to get too little space
Change to: rule card with 'flex flex-col gap-2'
Top row: rule name (font-medium, full width, allow wrap)
Bottom row: flex-row gap-2 with status badge + action buttons
Remove any fixed widths on rule name column that might cause word-stacking
Action buttons: 'flex gap-2 flex-wrap' to handle wrap if needed

userStories.title: Admin: Set 6 Homepage Highlights
userStories.description: The homepage highlights section shows featured menu items. Set exactly 6 items as homepage highlights in the admin panel. These should be the best-looking, most photogenic items that represent the cafe's signature dishes. Mark them as featured=true in the DB with the correct sort_order.

Run SQL: UPDATE menu_items SET featured = true WHERE code IN ('MT04', 'MT01', 'TM03', 'BF22', 'AP01', 'DD02') -- adjust codes to match actual codes
Run SQL: UPDATE menu_items SET featured = false WHERE code NOT IN (above list)
Verify with: SELECT code, name_en, featured FROM menu_items WHERE featured = true

Min highlights constant is MIN_HIGHLIGHTS = 6 in menu.ts — confirm this is set

userStories.title: Admin Alerts: Notify About Items Without Photos
userStories.description: In the admin panel, show a prominent alert/banner listing all menu items that have no photo (no image file in public/images/menu/{code}.jpg). This reminds the admin to upload photos. The alert should be dismissible per session but always re-appear on next visit.

Return list of items with missing photos
In AdminMenuTable component, fetch missing photos list on mount, show alert if list not empty
Use sessionStorage to persist 'dismissed' state so it reappears on next page visit
Alert style: bg-amber-50 border-amber-400 text-amber-800 with a warning icon

userStories.title: Admin: Non-POS Display Categories (New Items, Vegetarian, etc.)
userStories.description: Current categories are synced from the POS system (e.g., Rice, Noodles, Drinks). Add a separate set of 'display categories' that are website-only (not tied to POS): 'New Items', 'Vegetarian', 'Chef's Picks', 'Under RM15'. These display categories allow flexible menu curation without breaking the POS sync. Admin can assign menu items to display categories in addition to their POS categories.
acceptanceCriteria: A new 'Display Categories' section exists in the admin Categories tab

Items can belong to both a POS category AND display categories simultaneously

Add a junction table `item_display_categories` (item_id, display_category_id)

Update src/components/admin/admin-categories-panel.tsx to show both POS categories (read-only) and Display Categories (editable)
Update menu filter component to include display categories as filter tabs
Update getMenuItems() to accept optional display_category filter param

userStories.title: Admin: List All Items for a Particular Category
userStories.description: In the admin Menu tab, when filtering by a category, show all items belonging to that category clearly. Currently all items are shown in a flat list. Add a category filter/selector in the admin Menu tab that shows only items from the selected category, making it easier to manage items per category.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Menu tab has a category dropdown/filter at the top
Selecting a category shows only items belonging to that category
The item count per category is shown in the dropdown (e.g., 'Rice (8 items)')
An 'All Categories' option shows all items (default)

Add a <select> or tab filter component above the items list
Filter items client-side: filteredItems = activeCategory ? items.filter(i => i.categories.includes(activeCategory)) : items
Show item count: categories.map(c => ({ name: c, count: items.filter(i => i.categories.includes(c)).length }))
Pass categories prop (already available) to the component

userStories.title: Admin: Drag and Drop Items Between Categories
userStories.description: In the admin Menu tab (with category filter from US-032), allow drag-and-drop to move items from one category to another. Dragging an item and dropping it onto a different category reassigns it. This makes category management visual and intuitive.
acceptanceCriteria: Items in the admin menu list are draggable

Wrap admin menu table items in DraggableItem components

userStories.title: Admin: Duplicate Item to Multiple Categories
userStories.description: In the admin Menu tab, allow an item to be duplicated (added) to one or more additional categories. This is different from moving — the item appears in all assigned categories simultaneously. Useful for items like 'Iced Lemon Tea' that belong to both 'Drinks' and 'Lunch Sets' categories.

userStories.title: Menu Page: Dynamic Display Based on Time of Day
userStories.description: On the menu page, the default active category/filter should change based on the current time. Breakfast items (if available) are prioritized before 11AM, lunch items from 11AM-3PM, dinner items from 3PM-close. This is determined by the item's existing timeFrom/timeUntil fields. The admin can configure the time-based category priorities in /admin. Items outside their time window are still visible but shown lower in the list.
acceptanceCriteria: The default active category tab on /en/menu changes based on Malaysia time
If current time is lunch hours (11AM-3PM): 'Lunch Sets' or 'Rice' category is selected by default
If current time is dinner (3PM-10:30PM): 'Mains' or similar is default
A subtle 'Serving now' indicator shows which categories are currently available
Admin can configure time-based category priorities in a new 'Time Display' section in /admin
This does NOT hide any items — just changes the default selected filter
Verify: at lunch time, menu page loads with lunch category pre-selected
technicalNotes: In menu page server component: get current Malaysia time (UTC+8), determine which time-slot is active
Create a simple time-slot config in src/lib/time-slots.ts: { breakfast: { start: '07:00', end: '11:00', defaultCategory: 'Breakfast' }, lunch: ..., dinner: ... }
Pass defaultCategory to MenuGrid which passes to MenuFilter as initialCategory
Store time-slot config in DB or a JSON config file (data/time-slots.json) so admin can edit it
Add API route GET/POST /api/admin/time-slots for admin to configure
Add a new 'Time Settings' section in admin Categories tab

userStories.title: Operating Hours Alert for Non-Admin Users Outside Ordering Time
userStories.description: If a non-admin user views the menu page (or any page) outside operating hours (11AM-11PM, last order 10:30PM), show a friendly banner/alert informing them the cafe is currently closed. The operating hours are based on Malaysia time (UTC+8). This should be configured in the Rules system so admin can update the hours without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Outside 11AM-11PM Malaysia time: a non-dismissible banner appears at top of /en/menu: 'We're currently closed. Open daily 11AM-11PM. Last order 10:30PM.'
Inside 11AM-10:30PM: no alert, menu shows normally
Between 10:30PM-11PM: show 'Last order has passed for today. Come back tomorrow!'
Non-admin users see the alert; admin users do not (to allow editing anytime)
Operating hours are stored as a Rule in the Rules system so admin can change them
Verify: manually change system time or mock time → alert appears outside hours
technicalNotes: Create a new Rule type 'operating_hours' or use a config in data/operating-hours.json
In menu page server component: get Malaysia time, check against operating hours, pass closedStatus to page
Add OperatingHoursAlert component: shown only when !isAdmin && closedStatus !== 'open'
Closed statuses: 'before_open' (before 11AM), 'after_last_order' (10:30PM-11PM), 'closed' (after 11PM)
Create admin UI in Rules tab to configure operating hours (start, last_order, close times)

userStories.title: AI Helper in Admin Rules Tab for Complex Rule Configuration
userStories.description: Rules can be complex (time-based, category-based, discount cascades). Add an AI helper in the Rules tab that admin can describe in natural language what they want ('disable all drinks after 7PM except lemon tea') and the AI suggests a rule configuration. The AI uses the existing rule schema to generate a pre-filled rule form.
acceptanceCriteria: The Rules tab has an 'AI Helper' button or collapsible panel at the top
Admin types a natural language rule description and clicks 'Generate Rule'
The AI returns a structured rule suggestion (ruleType, targetType, targetCategories, timeFrom, timeUntil, etc.)
The suggested rule is pre-filled into the 'Add Rule' form for admin to review and save
The AI understands the cafe context: knows category names, operating hours, item types
Verify: type 'Give 10% discount on all rice dishes at lunch time' → AI generates a discount rule pre-filled
technicalNotes: Create src/components/admin/admin-rules-ai-helper.tsx — chat input + suggested rule display
Create API route src/app/api/admin/rules/ai-suggest/route.ts
Use existing AI SDK (Groq/OpenRouter via src/lib/chat/provider.ts) with a rules-specific system prompt
System prompt: explain the Rule schema (RuleType: disable/discount/featured, TargetType: category/items, fields) and tell AI to return JSON
Parse AI JSON response and populate the rule form state
Include current categories in the AI context so it knows valid category names

userStories.title: Blog Post Content Cleanup: Remove Metadata Sections
userStories.description: The imported blog posts contain metadata sections like '## Post Type', '## Expected Engagement', '## Perspective', '## Response Strategy', '## Best Posting Time', '## References', '## Source'. These are internal content planning notes and should not be shown to cafe customers. Strip these sections from the rendered blog post content, showing only the main article body.
acceptanceCriteria: Blog detail pages do NOT show any of: Post Type, Expected Engagement, Perspective, Response Strategy, Best Posting Time, References, Source sections
The post intro metadata block (Type/Created/Tags frontmatter text) is also stripped
The main content (the actual post copy, story, or announcement) renders cleanly
Blog listing excerpts also show clean content (first paragraph of actual content)
Verify: click any blog post → no metadata headers visible, only the actual content
technicalNotes: In src/lib/blog-local.ts — after parsing markdown, apply a cleanContent() function
Strip sections by regex: remove everything from a ## heading that matches the metadata list through to the next ## or end of string
Regex pattern: /\n##\s+(Post Type|Expected Engagement|Perspective|Response Strategy|Best Posting Time|References|Source|Content)[\s\S]*?(?=\n##|$)/gi
Also strip YAML-like lines: /^\*\*(Type|Created|Tags|Status|Notion):\*\*.*$/gm
Also strip the first frontmatter section (the --- ... --- block at top) before the actual content
Test with 2-3 post files to confirm clean output before committing

userStories.title: Add Tests for All New Features (US-011 to US-038) + Fix Errors
userStories.description: Extend the admin Tests tab (US-009) with new tests covering all features added in US-011 through US-038. Run the tests programmatically. For any failing tests, write the error as a new user story in prd.json and continue fixing. This creates a self-healing development loop.
acceptanceCriteria: New test cases added in src/lib/tests/ for each new feature: chef pick card, bottom nav, language icons, blog cleanup, recipe modal close, admin mobile, etc.
All smoke tests pass (all pages return 200)
All unit tests pass (blog content cleanup, time-slot logic, operating hours check)
At least 80% of integration tests pass
Any failing tests have their root cause identified and a new user story added to prd.json
Test results visible in /admin > Tests tab
Verify: 'Run All' in Tests tab shows green for most tests
technicalNotes: Add new test cases to src/lib/tests/smoke.ts: test /en/menu filter, /en/blog detail page, /admin/chat-settings, /en/about
Add to src/lib/tests/unit.ts: test cleanContent() strips metadata, test getMalaysiaTime() returns UTC+8, test operating hours check function
Add to src/lib/tests/integration.ts: test GET /api/admin/highlights, GET /api/admin/display-categories, GET /api/admin/time-slots
Add to src/lib/tests/e2e.ts: test recipe modal close button visible on mobile, test language switcher in mobile header, test 'Added' button state
If any test fails: append a new US-040+ story to prd.json with error details and fix steps
Use the 'Generate Stories' button in Tests tab to auto-create stories for failures

userStories.title: Chrome DevTools + Agent Browser Verification for All New Features
userStories.description: Use Chrome DevTools MCP tools and/or the agent-browser skill to visually verify all features from US-011 to US-038 are working correctly in the browser. Capture screenshots for key views. Fix any visual regressions or console errors found.
acceptanceCriteria: Take screenshot of /en/menu on mobile (390px) — chef pick visible at top, bottom nav visible, no horizontal scroll
Take screenshot of /en/blog — clean posts, no metadata, featured post at top
Take screenshot of /en/about — cover page image visible, paintings image visible

Take screenshot of /admin/rules on mobile (390px) — rule names horizontal, no clipping
No console errors on any page

technicalNotes: Use mcp__chrome-devtools__navigate_page, mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot, mcp__chrome-devtools__list_console_messages
Use mcp__chrome-devtools__emulate to set mobile viewport (width: 390, height: 844)
Use mcp__chrome-devtools__resize_page for desktop view (1280x800)
